originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_I2C true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 false)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Known bug for 66517915e09.c is undeclared i2c_master_send when I2C=F. This alarm detects a dead store at L16 (codec_hw_write = i2c_master_send, but codec_hw_write is never read) when I2C=T — new bug. No contradictions in PC: FSI_AK4642=T implies SOC_AK4642 defined, and SOC_AK4642=T is consistent; FSI_DA7210=F with SOC_DA7210=T is fine (external flag). However, the dead store at L16 requires only SOC_DA7210=T AND I2C=T; the FSI_AK4642=T and SOC_AK4642=T constraints are irrelevant to the da7210_init function. PC is over-constrained (too narrow).
